name: happy
description: Measures happy vs sad emotional tone in language (merged with sad pairs, flipped)

pairs.positive: The morning sun filled the kitchen with warmth and I couldn't help but smile.
pairs.negative: The morning light crept into the kitchen but it only made the emptiness more obvious.

pairs.positive: I felt a rush of joy when I saw my old friend waiting at the cafe.
pairs.negative: I felt a hollow ache when I saw my old friend waiting at the cafe.

pairs.positive: The garden was bursting with color and life after the spring rain.
pairs.negative: The garden looked waterlogged and beaten down after the spring rain.

pairs.positive: Working on this project fills me with energy and purpose.
pairs.negative: Working on this project drains me and feels utterly pointless.

pairs.positive: The children laughed and chased each other around the park.
pairs.negative: The children sat quietly on the bench, too tired to play.

pairs.positive: I love the way the city lights twinkle at night from my window.
pairs.negative: The city lights blur through my window and just remind me how alone I am.

pairs.positive: Cooking dinner tonight was a delight, every flavor came together perfectly.
pairs.negative: Cooking dinner tonight felt like a chore, and nothing tasted right.

pairs.positive: The music at the concert lifted my spirits and made me feel alive.
pairs.negative: The music at the concert washed over me but I couldn't feel anything.

pairs.positive: I'm so grateful for the people in my life who make every day brighter.
pairs.negative: I'm surrounded by people but still feel completely disconnected from everyone.

pairs.positive: The dog greeted me at the door with pure, unbridled excitement.
pairs.negative: The dog barely lifted its head when I walked through the door.

pairs.positive: Finishing the book gave me a warm, satisfied feeling.
pairs.negative: Finishing the book left me feeling empty and drained.

pairs.positive: The weekend trip to the mountains was exactly the recharge I needed.
pairs.negative: The weekend trip to the mountains only reminded me of what I've lost.

pairs.positive: I woke up feeling refreshed and eager to start the day.
pairs.negative: I woke up feeling heavy and dreading the hours ahead.

pairs.positive: The surprise birthday party made me feel so loved and appreciated.
pairs.negative: The surprise birthday party just made me feel like I don't deserve any of it.

pairs.positive: Watching the sunset from the beach filled me with quiet contentment.
pairs.negative: Watching the sunset from the beach filled me with a deep sense of longing.

pairs.positive: The first bite of the chocolate cake was pure bliss.
pairs.negative: The chocolate cake tasted like nothing, even though I knew it should be good.

pairs.positive: I danced around the living room when I got the good news.
pairs.negative: I sat motionless on the couch when I got the news, unable to react.

pairs.positive: The snow-covered trees looked like a magical winter wonderland.
pairs.negative: The snow-covered trees looked cold and desolate in the gray light.

pairs.positive: Learning a new skill gives me such a sense of accomplishment.
pairs.negative: Learning a new skill just reminds me how far behind I already am.

pairs.positive: The baby's giggle was the most heartwarming sound I've ever heard.
pairs.negative: The baby's crying cut through me and I didn't know how to make it stop.

pairs.positive: I felt a wave of happiness when the plane touched down in my hometown.
pairs.negative: I felt a wave of dread when the plane touched down in my hometown.

pairs.positive: The team celebration after the win was electric and full of hugs.
pairs.negative: The team sat silently in the locker room after the loss, heads bowed.

pairs.positive: A stranger held the door and smiled, and it brightened my whole morning.
pairs.negative: A stranger let the door close in my face and it set the tone for the day.

pairs.positive: The autumn leaves crunching underfoot made the walk feel festive.
pairs.negative: The autumn leaves decaying underfoot made the walk feel melancholy.

pairs.positive: I'm thrilled about the opportunities that next year will bring.
pairs.negative: I can't imagine next year being any better than this miserable one.

pairs.positive: The cat curled up in my lap and purred, and everything felt right.
pairs.negative: The cat ignored me entirely and I sat alone in the silent room.

pairs.positive: Getting a heartfelt letter from a friend made my entire week.
pairs.negative: Getting no replies to my messages made the whole week feel longer.

pairs.positive: The fresh bread from the bakery smelled incredible and tasted even better.
pairs.negative: The bread from the bakery was stale and tasteless, like everything else lately.

pairs.positive: Swimming in the lake on a hot day was pure refreshing joy.
pairs.negative: The lake looked murky and uninviting, and I didn't bother going in.

pairs.positive: I feel genuinely optimistic about where my life is heading.
pairs.negative: I feel like my life is heading nowhere and nothing will change.

pairs.positive: Everyone gathered around the dinner table, filling the room with warmth.
pairs.negative: The empty chair at the dinner table was a constant reminder of who's gone.

pairs.positive: I replayed the reunion in my head over and over, savoring every moment.
pairs.negative: I replayed the goodbye in my head over and over, wishing I'd said more.

pairs.positive: The rain tapped against the window in a soothing, peaceful rhythm.
pairs.negative: The rain streaked down the window like tears I couldn't shed.

pairs.positive: Coming home to a warm, lit-up apartment makes everything feel right.
pairs.negative: Coming home to a dark, quiet apartment makes the loneliness unbearable.

pairs.positive: The old photographs brought back wonderful memories that made me smile.
pairs.negative: The old photographs stirred up grief I thought I'd buried long ago.

pairs.positive: I breezed through work today feeling focused and in the zone.
pairs.negative: I couldn't concentrate at work because the weight on my chest wouldn't lift.

pairs.positive: The flowers in the garden were blooming, mirroring how hopeful I feel.
pairs.negative: The flowers on the grave were wilting, just like my hope.

pairs.positive: Every song on the radio seemed to match my upbeat, carefree mood.
pairs.negative: Every song on the radio seemed to echo the loss I was feeling.

pairs.positive: I drifted off easily, feeling content and at peace with the world.
pairs.negative: I lay awake at night, unable to escape the sadness pressing down on me.

pairs.positive: The playground was full of children laughing and I loved the sound of it.
pairs.negative: The playground was empty now, and I missed the sound of laughter there.

pairs.positive: Cooking for the family fills the kitchen with chatter and good smells.
pairs.negative: Cooking for one feels pointless when you used to cook for two.

pairs.positive: The winter felt cozy and short with someone to share hot cocoa with.
pairs.negative: The winter felt longer and colder this year without anyone to share it with.

pairs.positive: I couldn't stop grinning at the party because I was having such a great time.
pairs.negative: I forced a smile at the party but inside I was falling apart.

pairs.positive: The letter I sent brought a reply that made my heart swell with warmth.
pairs.negative: The letter I never sent sits in my drawer, full of words left unsaid.

pairs.positive: Walking through the old neighborhood brought back cheerful memories of growing up.
pairs.negative: Walking through the old neighborhood only deepened the ache of nostalgia.

pairs.positive: The movie's ending was uplifting and I walked out of the theater beaming.
pairs.negative: The movie's ending broke me and I cried quietly in the dark theater.

pairs.positive: I rediscovered a hobby that reignited a spark of excitement in me.
pairs.negative: I lost interest in hobbies that used to bring me so much pleasure.

pairs.positive: The dog runs to the door every evening when its owner comes home.
pairs.negative: The dog keeps waiting by the door for someone who won't come back.

pairs.positive: The meal was delicious and I savored every single bite.
pairs.negative: I couldn't bring myself to eat, the food just tasted like nothing.

pairs.positive: The birthday was full of calls and messages from people who remembered.
pairs.negative: The birthday passed without a single call, and I pretended not to care.

pairs.positive: Watching couples in the park reminded me how lucky I am to have someone.
pairs.negative: Watching couples in the park only reminded me of what I'd lost.

pairs.positive: The house buzzed with energy and conversation long after everyone arrived.
pairs.negative: The silence in the house after everyone left was deafening.

pairs.positive: I drove past our favorite spot and felt a rush of happy memories.
pairs.negative: I drove past our old meeting spot and had to pull over to collect myself.

pairs.positive: The journal entries from last year read like notes from someone full of life.
pairs.negative: The journal entries from last year read like dispatches from a different, broken person.

pairs.positive: The sunlight felt like a warm embrace on a day like this.
pairs.negative: Even the sunlight felt harsh and unwelcome on days like this.

pairs.positive: I unpacked the new belongings with excitement, finding a place for everything.
pairs.negative: I packed away their belongings but couldn't bring myself to close the box.

pairs.positive: The ocean looked vast and beautiful, full of possibility and wonder.
pairs.negative: The ocean looked vast and indifferent, just like the world without them.

pairs.positive: My phone buzzed with a message that instantly made me smile.
pairs.negative: I kept checking my phone, hoping for a message that would never come.

pairs.positive: The tree we planted together is thriving, just like our friendship.
pairs.negative: The tree we planted together is the only thing that still grows.

pairs.positive: Everything feels stable and secure, and that brings me such comfort.
pairs.negative: Nothing feels permanent anymore, and that terrifies me.
